abilities.name: Blessed
abilities.description: Power Points: 15; Powers: Barrier (Savage Worlds: Adventure Edition p155), Holy Symbol (Deadlands - The Weird West p76), Protection (Savage Worlds: Adventure Edition p165-166)

abilities.name: Sinnin’
abilities.description: Blessed who violate their core beliefs (see Sin on page 56) are forsaken by whatever forces of good grant them their powers. Minor sins give the character −2 to his Faith rolls for a week. Major sins rob him of all arcane powers for a week. Mortal sins cause the character to be forsaken until the penitent hero completes some great quest or task of atonement to regain his lost powers.

powers.name: Barrier
powers.summary: Creates 10 yard long, 2 yard tall, Toughness 10 barrier. -
powers.description:
Barrier creates a straight wall 5′′ (10 yards)
long and 1′′ (two yards) tall, of immobile
material that conforms to the surface it’s cast
upon. Thickness varies depending on what
the wall is made of, but is usually a few inches.
The wall has a Hardness of 10, and may be
destroyed as any other object (see Breaking
Things on page 98).

When the spell expires or the wall is broken
it crumbles to dust or dissipates. Trap­pings
are never left behind.
powerModifiers: DAMAGE (+1): The barrier causes 2d4 damage to anyone who contacts it.
SHAPED (+2): The barrier forms a circle, square or other basic shape.
SIZE (+1): The length and height of the barrier doubles.
megaPowerOptions: STALWART WALLS (+2): The barrier gains +12 MDC Armor.
powers.descriptionHTML:
Barrier creates a straight wall 5′′ (10 yards) long and 1′′ (two yards) tall, of immobile material that conforms to the surface it’s cast upon. Thickness varies depending on what the wall is made of, but is usually a few inches. The wall has a Hardness of 10, and may be destroyed as any other object (see Breaking Things on page 98).

When the spell expires or the wall is broken it crumbles to dust or dissipates. Trap­pings are never left behind.

Modifiers

  • Damage (+1): The barrier causes 2d4 damage to anyone who contacts it.
  • Shaped (+2): The barrier forms a circle, square or other basic shape.
  • Size (+1): The length and height of the barrier doubles.
  • Strong (+1): The wall is Toughness 12.

Savage Worlds: Adventure Edition p.155

powers.name: Holy Symbol
powers.summary: Supernatural evil cannot touch you unless Spirit roll as free action (-2 on raise), requires a holy symbol. -
powerModifiers: AREA EFFECT (+2/+3): For +2 points the power affects all allies within a Medium Blast Template centered on the caster. It does not prevent creatures from entering the area, only their attacks against those within it. For +3 points the area of effect is increased to a Large Blast Template.
STRONG (+1): Spirit rolls by the creature are made at −2 (–4 with a raise).
powers.descriptionHTML:
Modifiers

  • Area Effect (+2/+3): For +2 points the power affects all allies within a Medium Blast Template centered on the caster. It does not prevent creatures from entering the area, only their attacks against those within it. For +3 points the area of effect is increased to a Large Blast Template.
  • Strong (+1): Spirit rolls by the creature are made at −2 (–4 with a raise).

Deadlands - The Weird West p.76

powers.name: Protection
powers.description:
Protection creates a field of energy or armor
around a character, giving him 2 points of
Armor, or +4 with a raise.

Whether the protection is visible or not
depends on the Trapping — this is entirely
up to the caster.

Protection stacks with all other armor, natural
or worn, and is negated by AP as usual.
powerModifiers: ADDITIONAL RECIPIENTS (+1): The power may affect additional targets at a cost of 1 Power Point each.
MORE ARMOR (+1): Success grants 4 points of Armor (+6 with a raise).
TOUGHNESS (+1): Protection provides Toughness instead of Armor and is not affected by AP (magical or otherwise). This means it stacks with natural or worn armor.
megaPowerOptions: GREATER PROTECTION (+3): Protection provides 6 points of MDC Armor (+8 with a raise), which stacks with all other armor, natural or worn. May be combined with the Toughness Power Modifier, but not More Armor.
powers.descriptionHTML:
Protection creates a field of energy or armor around a character, giving him 2 points of Armor, or +4 with a raise.

Whether the protection is visible or not depends on the Trapping — this is entirely up to the caster.

Protection stacks with all other armor, natural or worn, and is negated by AP as usual.

Modifiers

  • Additional Recipients (+1): The power may affect additional targets at a cost of 1 Power Point each.
  • More Armor (+1): Success grants 4 points of Armor (+6 with a raise).
  • Toughness (+1): Protection provides Toughness instead of Armor and is not affected by AP (magical or otherwise). This means it stacks with natural or worn armor.

Savage Worlds: Adventure Edition p.165-166

background:
Dusty McCaw was raised on his parents' ranch outside a tight-knit Mormon settlement in Deseret. From a young age he had a quiet curiosity about things the sermons did not explain. He asked travelers questions, kept strange pamphlets, and experimented with charms and remedies that sat somewhere between folk medicine and forbidden study. Most folks tolerated it as harmless eccentricity until the plague hit. 

The Plague began with Dusty's cattle. When the plague spread to neighboring ranches, whispers turned to accusation. By the time townsfolk began falling ill, fear needed a scapegoat, and Dusty's curiosity made him an easy target. Leadership did not drag him out in chains; they told him, clearly and publicly, that he and his family must leave for the good of the community. No trial was had, and no proof presented.

The fever followed them into exile. He watched his wife and children die one by one, scouring every scrap of occult knowledge he possessed in a desperate attempt to call them back. None of them answered. His last companion was his horse, the only living thing that remained beside him, and when it too fell to the sickness and died at his side, something in Dusty broke clean. He gave up on raising the dead that day. From then on, he turned all his study toward healing and prevention, vowing that if death came again, he would meet it before it could claim another soul.
description:
Dusty McCaw is sixty-one years old and looks every day of it, though not in a fragile way. He is lean rather than frail, with the kind of wiry strength that comes from decades of fence mending, saddle lifting, and long days in the sun. His shoulders are slightly rounded from years in the saddle. One knee stiffens when the weather turns cold, and he stands up slow, but once he's moving, he moves steadily.

His face is narrow and weather-cut, deep lines carved around the mouth and eyes. His beard is more gray than brown now, trimmed short when he has the chance, left uneven when he does not. His hands are calloused and cracked, permanently stained with dust and oil. His clothes are clean when possible but worn thin: an old hat reshaped more times than it should have survived, a faded coat with careful patches, boots that have been resoled twice.

gear.name: Lantern
gear.notes: Provides light in a 4” radius for three hours per pint of oil. If it’s dropped there’s a 50% chance the lantern breaks, and a 1-in-6 chance it sets normal combustibles alight.
gear.summary: Provides light in a 4” radius for three hours per pint of oil. If it’s dropped there’s a 50% chance the lantern breaks, and a 1-in-6 chance it sets normal combustibles alight.

gear.name: Restoration elixir
gear.notes: The drinker recovers all Fatigue levels.
gear.summary: The drinker recovers all Fatigue levels.

journal.text:
We rode out to the old homestead where the office is meant to stand. Travis Morcy, Doc Brown, Jolene Reeves, Eleanor Finch, myself, and a quiet fellow whose name I never did catch. Place looked worn down but still upright, like it was waiting to see if we meant to stay.

Travis wasted no time. Walked straight up, slipped through a window like he owned the place, and before I’d even finished counting my breath there were four shots echoing through the boards. A caterwaul came tearing out the back, bleeding and furious, smashing shutters clean off as it fled. It ran hard into the dark. Wounded, but not dead. Things like that don’t quit easy.

Doc put the window right again, good work there. Then he tried his hand at building a toaster of all things. The window holds. The toaster does not. He and Travis took turns cranking at it, but neither could spin the shaft quick enough to warm the coils. I reckon some things are better left to steady fires than spinning gears.

Eleanor found a barn close by and settled her horse, Sassifrass, inside. Doc lined the entrance with bear traps in case that caterwaul decides it’s got unfinished business. Smart precaution. Wounded beasts remember.

As for me, I laid my bedroll beneath a tree just off the house. No sense crowding the others. I’ve slept under worse skies than this one. If that creature comes back in the night, I expect I’ll hear it before most.

name: Dusty McCaw

weapons.name: Lariat
weapons.notes: -1 Parry, Reach 2, Used to initiate a Test using the wielder’s Fighting skill. The target is Entangled with success, Bound on a raise.
